compressed_view: UX spec structure: 9 required sections — Overview/Scope, Screen Inventory/Stack Order, Component Catalogue, Token Catalogue, State Coverage, Accessibility Requirements, Motion/Animation, Analytics Instrumentation, Principle Application Table. Compliance gateway checks 5 dimensions (token, component, motion, accessibility, pattern compliance) — must be 5/5 pass. Heuristic validation checks all 13 UX Foundations principles (12/12 pass + 1 N/A allowed). Separate ux-research.md documents principle analysis; ux-spec.md is the specification. Phase 3 also produces build prompts (ux-build.md + design-build.md). New features (no v1) still require non-skippable Phase 3 with ux-spec.md before any view code. Stack order pattern: primary action first, then health signals, then progress tracking.

task_signature.context: What a complete ux-spec.md contains. Structure derived from Home v2 (9-section spec) and Training v2 UX deliverables. Used by /ux skill in Phase 3.

learned_patterns.pattern: Required ux-spec.md sections (9-section structure from Home v2)
learned_patterns.decision: Every ux-spec.md must contain these 9 sections: (1) Overview & Scope, (2) Screen Inventory & Stack Order, (3) Component Catalogue, (4) Token Catalogue, (5) State Coverage, (6) Accessibility Requirements, (7) Motion & Animation, (8) Analytics Instrumentation, (9) Principle Application Table. Home v2 spec passed 5/5 compliance gateway and 12/12 heuristic validation.

section_details.1_overview_scope: Feature name, work_subtype (v2_refactor or new), audit finding count by priority (P0/P1/P2), in-scope vs deferred findings, estimated effort
section_details.2_screen_inventory: Stack order for scrollable screens (e.g. Home v2: Toolbar → Greeting → ReadinessCard → Training & Nutrition → Status → Goal → Metrics). Decision rationale for scroll constraint.
section_details.3_component_catalogue: Named components from AppComponents.swift that will be used. For v2 refactors: which existing components are reused, which new ones are being promoted (e.g. AppMetricColumn, AppMetricTile promoted in Home v2 T4/T5). Private sub-views declared in the v2 file.
section_details.4_token_catalogue: Explicit list of AppColor.*, AppText.*, AppSpacing.*, AppSize.*, AppCornerRadius.*, AppMotion.* tokens used. New tokens being added to AppTheme.swift (e.g. Home v2 T1: AppText.monoCaption; Training v2: AppSize.tabBarClearance, AppText.monoCaption).
section_details.5_state_coverage: Every UI state the screen can be in: loading (skeleton), empty, error, partial data, full data, offline. Spec must say which component/view handles each state. Home v2 had dedicated HomeEmptyStateView (T10).
section_details.6_accessibility: VoiceOver labels for all interactive elements (minimum 30+ for complex screens). Dynamic Type scaling at AX5. Minimum 44pt tap targets. Home v2 T11: 30+ labels. Training v2: Full AX5 support.
section_details.7_motion_animation: Which interactions have transitions. Tokens: AppSpring, AppEasing, motionSafe (added in Home v2 T2). Rest timer animation (Training v2 RestTimerView). Skeleton loading fade-in (Training v2 SkeletonLoadingView).
section_details.8_analytics: List all events being added, their screen prefix, parameters, consent gating. Home v2: 3 events (home_action_tap, home_action_completed, home_empty_state_shown) + 1 deferred (home_metric_tile_tap). Training v2: 12 training_* events.
section_details.9_principle_application_table: Two-column table: UX Foundations Principle | How honored in this spec. Must cover all 13 principles. 'N/A with justification' allowed. Home v2 ux-research.md covered 12/13 (1 N/A).

learned_patterns.pattern: Compliance gateway — 5 dimensions, must be 5/5 pass
learned_patterns.decision: The Phase 3 compliance gateway checks: (1) Token compliance — all visual values resolve to AppTheme tokens; (2) Component reuse — no ad-hoc components when an AppComponents.swift equivalent exists; (3) Motion — animations use AppSpring/AppEasing or motionSafe; (4) Accessibility — all interactive elements have accessibilityLabel/Hint, Dynamic Type works at AX5; (5) Pattern compliance — no raw literals, no v1 patterns carried forward. Home v2 result: 5/5 pass (motion = DS evolution item accepted). Failed gateways block Phase 4.
source_executions: home-today-screen state.json ux_or_integration.compliance_gateway = '5/5 pass (motion tokens = DS evolution)'

learned_patterns.decision: The heuristic validation checks all 13 UX Foundations principles: 1.1 Fitts's Law, 1.2 Hick's Law, 1.3 Visual Hierarchy, 1.4 Data Viz, 1.5 Feedback & Status, 1.6 Error Recovery, 1.7 Dark Mode, 1.8 Motion Safety, Parts 2-10 (IA, Interaction, Permission, State, A11y, Motion, Content, Platform). Each must be Pass or N/A with justification.

ux-research.md documents WHICH of the 13 principles apply to this specific surface and HOW each is honored. It is the analysis document; ux-spec.md is the specification document. Both are required deliverables. Home v2: ux-research.md mapped 12/13 principles. Both go in .claude/features/{name}/.

learned_patterns.pattern: Build prompts — auto-generated deliverables from Phase 3
learned_patterns.decision: Phase 3 must also produce: docs/prompts/{date}-{feature}-ux-build.md and docs/prompts/{date}-{feature}-design-build.md. These are handoff prompts for build execution. Home v2 generated both on 2026-04-09. They contain the full component spec, token list, and acceptance criteria needed for Phase 4 build.

learned_patterns.pattern: Stack order decision for Home-style screens
learned_patterns.decision: Home v2 canonical stack order: Toolbar → Greeting (+ LiveInfoStrip) → ReadinessCard → Training & Nutrition CTA card → Status card → Goal card → Metrics row. Scrollable. Drop the above-the-fold no-scroll constraint from v1. The pattern: primary action first (workout/meal), then health signals, then progress tracking.

learned_patterns.pattern: New feature UX spec (non-refactor) — non-skippable Phase 3
learned_patterns.decision: For brand-new screens (no v1 to refactor), file lives at canonical path (no v2/ subdir). Phase 3 is non-skippable — must produce ux-spec.md and pass compliance gateway BEFORE any view code. Phase 4 starts with ux-foundations.md checklist applied to spec first, then view code. 'Build first, audit later' is forbidden.
source_executions: CLAUDE.md V2 Rule — For new UI features built from scratch

anti_patterns.pattern: Skipping ux-research.md and going straight to ux-spec.md
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: ux-research.md is the analysis that justifies the spec decisions. Without it, the spec has no traceability to UX principles. Both documents are required by v2-refactor-checklist A2 and A3.
anti_patterns.source_execution: v2-refactor-checklist A2, A3 — both required before Phase 4

anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Missing empty, loading, and error states from the spec means they get omitted from Phase 4 implementation. Home v2 found F15/F16 (no empty state) as P0 findings — required dedicated HomeEmptyStateView (T10). Always spec all states explicitly.
anti_patterns.source_execution: home-today-screen v2 F15, F16 — P0 findings about empty state

anti_patterns.pattern: Analytics section with events missing screen prefix
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: If the analytics section of ux-spec.md lists unprefixed screen-scoped events, the PRD approval gate will reject them. The /analytics spec sub-command validates prefix compliance and refuses to write a non-compliant spec.
anti_patterns.source_execution: CLAUDE.md Analytics Naming Convention — enforcement rule 1

speedup_instructions: When /ux is invoked for Phase 3: use the 9-section structure as the scaffold for ux-spec.md. Run compliance gateway (5 dimensions) and heuristic validation (12/13 principles + 1 N/A allowed). Check section 8 analytics events against the ga4-event-spec.json cache entry for prefix compliance. Produce both ux-build.md and design-build.md prompts as Phase 3 closing deliverables. state.json ux_or_integration.type = 'ux' when done.
